title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
description: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a consumer-accessible version of its Mythos-class frontier model, available immediately to Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers at no extra charge through June 22. Fable 5 sets new state-of-the-art results across nearly all tested benchmarks and is priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — less than half the cost of Mythos Preview. New safety guardrails that block responses in high-risk domains such as cybersecurity and biology enabled the broad release of a previously restricted capability tier.

title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
description: Anthropic confidentially submitted a draft S-1 registration statement to the SEC, formally initiating the process for a proposed initial public offering of its common stock. The filing follows a similar move by OpenAI, which filed its own confidential S-1 on June 8, marking a pivotal moment as both frontier AI labs signal intentions to access public capital markets. No share count, price range, or expected timeline was disclosed in the confidential submission.

title: Apple unveils next generation of Apple Intelligence, Siri AI, and more
description: Apple announced a sweeping AI overhaul at WWDC 2026, rebranding its assistant as Siri AI with multi-turn conversation, real-time world knowledge, and deep cross-app context awareness embedded in the Dynamic Island across iOS 27 and macOS 27. New Apple Intelligence capabilities include AI-powered image editing in Photos, tab management in Safari, and a dedicated Siri app that preserves conversation context. The revamped Siri will launch as a beta for supported devices starting with English, with rapid language expansion planned.

title: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
description: Microsoft cut off access to dozens of its open source GitHub repositories after attackers injected password-stealing malware into projects widely used by AI developers, including Azure SDKs and tools integrated with Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and VS Code. The breach highlights supply-chain risks in the AI developer toolchain, where compromised packages can silently harvest credentials from engineers working across multiple AI platforms. Microsoft is actively investigating while urging developers to rotate credentials and audit recent dependency updates.

title: OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs Will Put AI Enthusiasm to the Test
description: With both OpenAI and Anthropic having filed confidential S-1 registration statements with the SEC within weeks of each other, Bloomberg examines whether public markets are prepared to value frontier AI companies whose capital requirements are extraordinary and whose revenue models remain unsettled. The dual filings represent a defining test of investor confidence in the AI sector's long-term economics beyond the current VC-fueled growth phase. Analysts note that pricing these businesses will require entirely new valuation frameworks given their non-traditional cost structures.

title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
description: Google has agreed to pay SpaceX approximately $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 for access to roughly 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs, CPUs, and supporting infrastructure. The deal illustrates the unprecedented compute scale required to train and serve frontier AI models and signals that hyperscalers are increasingly turning to unconventional partnerships to secure capacity. SpaceX's entry into the compute-leasing market represents a significant new revenue stream beyond its core aerospace operations.

title: Microsoft unveils new AI models to lessen reliance on OpenAI and lower costs for developers
description: At Build 2026, Microsoft unveiled MAI-Code-1-Flash, its first proprietary code-generation model that converts natural-language descriptions into application source code, as part of a broader effort to reduce dependence on OpenAI and offer more affordable AI options to developers. The announcement positions Microsoft as a direct competitor to Anthropic and Google in the AI coding model segment, a market it had previously ceded while OpenAI's Codex powered GitHub Copilot. Additional models targeting cost-sensitive enterprise workloads were also announced alongside new GitHub agent features.
